element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Wall/ceiling climbing and bell throwing; designated final leader
Brief flight, charged fire breath, icy surface traction
Spike walking, omnidirectional powerful attacks, slow movement
Speed, narrow passage navigation, wall/ceiling climbing, bomb attacks
Strategic tool for stage progression; each character solves different environmental puzzles

element_introduction_order.first_appears: End of each stage (13 levels per stage, boss after)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Character-specific weaknesses and advantages; requires tactical switching
Environmental navigation requiring specific character abilities

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Throughout stages (no specific first appearance documented)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Hearts (health), crystal orbs (life bar capacity), potions (full restore), extra lives
Normal unlocks true final boss; affects Ta-Keed encounter

typical_relief_triggers: Potion recovery (full health restore, one per character)
typical_tension_triggers: Boss encounters requiring tactical character switching
Character-specific death mechanics (non-Samson death within stage; Samson death = full restart)
Limited potion availability (one per character capacity)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Tutorial stages (1-4) establish individual character mechanics in low-pressure environments; subsequent stages escalate through enemy density, environmental complexity, and boss difficulty; character death stakes create tension between individual character survival and team progression

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Branching paths within stages allowing exploration of alternative routes; character-specific abilities unlock different pathways

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial stages (1-4) teach individual character mechanics in isolation with minimal threat; player selects order of character introduction
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Stages 5-16 introduce mandatory character switching; boss encounters require tactical ability selection; environmental complexity increases
difficulty_curve.late_game: Final stages escalate boss difficulty; true final boss (Ta-Keed) only accessible on Normal difficulty; team coordination and character resource management critical
notable_spikes: Transition from character-specific stages to multi-character boss stages
Introduction of mandatory character switching for stage progression
Final boss encounter gating (Easy vs Normal mode)

notable_level_patterns.description: Stages 1-4 each feature a single playable character with enemies and obstacles tailored to teach that character's mechanics (climbing for Samson, flight for Kikira, spike-walking for Gamm, speed/bombs for K.O.); no mandatory switching required
Stages 5 onward require player to switch between characters during progression and boss encounters; environmental puzzles and boss weaknesses leverage specific character abilities
Each stage contains 13 levels with multiple routes, allowing alternative progression based on active character selection and player choice

sources_used: Wikipedia - Little Samson (gameplay, character abilities, stage structure, difficulty modes)
The Cutting Room Floor - Little Samson (development team, regional differences)
